div.h1: Lock/Wait Ordering: Blocking Read — Correct vs. Deadlock

p.strong: FIFO buffer, single mutex (mydevice_mutex), two wait queues

div.h2: Mandatory Recheck (Step ⑩) — Why It Cannot Be Omitted

ul.li: Writer adds 64 bytes → wake_up_interruptible wakes ALL 4 readers
Reader 0 wins the mutex first → consumes 64 bytes → buffer_data_len = 0
Readers 1, 2, 3 acquire mutex in turn → find buffer_data_len == 0
Without recheck: readers access to_copy = min(count, 0) = 0; return 0 (EOF confusion)

With recheck: readers detect empty → release mutex → return -EAGAIN → caller retries Key invariant: Every wakeup from a wait queue is a hint, not a guarantee. The condition must always be verified under the protecting lock.
